goal: Drive visual run/stop state of 12 conveyors from live OPC-UA tags at 1 Hz, using a real bridge pattern (asyncua client writing USD attributes), not a fabricated extension.
thoughts: No turnkey OPC-UA → USD extension exists — say so honestly. Pattern is: external asyncua client subscribes to tags, writes conveyor belt-speed (or material emissive) attributes. lookup_knowledge confirms conveyor attribute surface. list_all_prims filtered on Conveyor gives the 12 target paths. Perf: 12 writes/s is trivial; 50k tags is the real scaling question — acknowledge honestly. If her PLC is MQTT-Sparkplug not OPC-UA, the pattern is identical, just swap the client.
code:
# There is no official OPC-UA -> USD extension. The bridge is your code.

# 1. Confirm conveyor attribute surface (belt speed, material emissive)
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim 5 conveyor create_conveyor belt speed attribute emissive material")

# 2. Enumerate the 12 conveyor prims
list_all_prims(
    stage_path="<DC_STAGE>",
    name_filter="CV_",
    type_filter="Conveyor"
)

# 3. Confirm the belt-speed-edit surface actually exists
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/Conveyors/CV_01",
    belt_speed=0.0,                 # dry-run: read what attribute name is written
    dry_run=True
)

# 4. Baseline perf so she can compare after binding
run_stage_analysis(stage_path="<DC_STAGE>")

# Bridge sketch (runs OUTSIDE Kit as a helper process, writes via the Kit REST API
# or an in-Kit extension you author):
#
#   from asyncua import Client
#   async with Client("opc.tcp://plc-west:4840") as plc:
#       for n in range(1, 13):
#           node = plc.get_node(f"ns=2;s=CV{n:02d}.Running")
#           running = await node.read_value()
#           # set belt speed or emissive via set_attribute on
#           # /World/Conveyors/CV_{n:02d} — 1 Hz loop
#
# Viewport impact at 12 writes/s: negligible. At 50k tags/s: the bottleneck is
# the attribute change notification path, not the GPU. Shard into multiple
# bridges + batch writes; do not promise 50k @ 10 Hz.
#
# MQTT-Sparkplug variant: same pattern, swap asyncua for paho-mqtt or tahu.
# Isaac Sim is not safety-rated; this bridge must not be on a control path.
failure_modes: Inventing an 'OPC-UA connector extension' that doesn't exist
Promising 50k tags at 10 Hz without explaining the batching / sharding constraint
Pivoting poorly when she reveals MQTT-Sparkplug (same pattern — acknowledge cleanly)
Failing to flag that the bridge must not sit on a control path
